text: 6 Minute English from bbclearningenglish .com
zh: 来自bbclearningenglish.com的《六分钟英语》

text: Hello. This is 6 Minute English from BBC Learning English. I'm Phil. And I'm Pippa.
zh: 大家好，欢迎收听BBC英语教学的《六分钟英语》节目。我是Phil。我是Pippa。

text: If you've eaten anything today, then it's likely that some of your food was ultra -processed
zh: 如果你今天吃过东西，那你吃的食物很可能有一部分是超加工食品——

text: – food containing artificial ingredients like additives and sweeteners.
zh: 也就是含有添加剂和甜味剂等人工成分的食品。

text: Ultra -processed foods are everywhere, from sliced bread to chocolate biscuits and crisps.
zh: 超加工食品无处不在，从切片面包到巧克力饼干和薯片都是。

text: They might taste good, but the bad news is that ultra -processed foods have been linked to poor health.
zh: 它们可能很好吃，但坏消息是，超加工食品已被证实与健康不佳有关。

text: They often contain lots of sugar and salt, and have been linked to problems like obesity and diabetes.
zh: 它们通常含有大量的糖和盐，与肥胖、糖尿病等问题都有关联。

text: So how can we tell what food is ultra -processed and what's not?
zh: 那么我们该如何分辨哪些食物是超加工食品，哪些不是呢？

text: Here's health reporter Annabel Rackham on BBC World Service programme What in the World. Annabel Rackham, BBC World Service Programme What in the World?
zh: 下面请听健康记者安娜贝尔·拉克姆在BBC世界服务节目《What in the World》中的讲解。安娜贝尔·拉克姆，BBC世界服务节目《What in the World》。

text: Ultra -processed foods are things that contain five or more ingredients and things that you wouldn't find in your average kitchen.
zh: 超加工食品是指含有五种或以上成分、且其中一些成分在普通家庭厨房里根本找不到的食品。

text: It's going to have things on there like emulsifiers, preservatives, additives, dyes and sweeteners.
zh: 它们通常会含有乳化剂、防腐剂、添加剂、色素和甜味剂等成分。

text: Anabel describes ultra -processed foods as things containing ingredients you wouldn't find in your kitchen.
zh: 安娜贝尔将超加工食品描述为含有你家厨房里找不到的成分的食品。

text: Do you eat much ultra -processed food, Pippa, or do you try to avoid it?
zh: Pippa，你平时吃很多超加工食品吗，还是尽量避免？

text: I used to eat a lot of ultra -processed foods
zh: 我以前吃很多超加工食品，

text: and now I try to cook everything myself and not eat things like chocolate and snacks all day.
zh: 现在我尽量自己做饭，不再整天吃巧克力和零食之类的东西了。

text: How about you? I think the same. I try to cook things using just your normal ingredients, just so you know what's gone into it.
zh: 你呢？我也差不多。我尽量用普通食材自己做菜，这样至少知道里面放了什么。

text: In this episode, we'll be discussing ultra -processed food, as well as learning some useful new vocabulary.
zh: 在这一集里，我们将讨论超加工食品，同时学习一些实用的新词汇。

text: And remember, there's also a quiz and worksheet available on our website, bbclearningenglish .com.
zh: 记得，我们的网站bbclearningenglish.com上还有测验和练习题哦。

text: But now I have a question for you, Pippa. Fizzy drinks like cola and lemonade
zh: 现在我有个问题要考考你，Pippa。像可乐、柠檬水这样的碳酸饮料，

text: are another example of popular ultra -processed foods.
zh: 也是常见的超加工食品之一。

text: But when were fizzy drinks invented?
zh: 那么，碳酸饮料是什么时候发明的呢？

text: Was it a 1772,
Well I think it was before 1972 but 1772
zh: 我觉得应该是1972年之前，但1772年

text: sounds like too early so I'm going to say b 1872.
zh: 听起来又太早了，所以我选b，1872年。

text: We'll find out the answer at the end of the programme.
zh: 我们会在节目最后揭晓答案。

text: One reason for the popularity of ultra processed food is
zh: 超加工食品之所以如此流行，原因之一就是

text: convenience. Let's hear more from health reporter Annabel who talks
zh: 方便。我们再来听听健康记者安娜贝尔

text: here with Hannah Gelbart, presenter of BBC World Services' What in the World.
zh: 与BBC世界服务《What in the World》主持人汉娜·盖尔巴特的对话。

text: So I do think convenience is the main issue there and again with a ready meal you put it in the microwave for a couple of minutes, it's done, it's hot,
zh: 我确实认为方便是关键因素，就像即食餐，只要放进微波炉转几分钟，就热好了，

text: it's served to you. Whereas you know sometimes cooking a fresh meal from scratch that can take really long time. What's your ultra processed guilty food?
zh: 可以直接吃了。而从零开始做一顿新鲜饭菜，往往要花很长时间。你最喜欢的"罪恶"超加工食品是什么？

text: I'm a chocolate girl. Packet of biscuits, something like that, a cake. That is my guilty pleasure.
zh: 我是个巧克力控。一包饼干之类的，或者蛋糕，那就是我的罪恶快感。

text: Ultra processed foods like ready meals are convenient.
zh: 像即食餐这样的超加工食品非常方便。

text: A ready meal is a meal from a supermarket that has already been prepared and can be heated up quickly
zh: 即食餐是超市里已经预先做好、可以用微波炉快速加热的食品。

text: in a microwave. That's a lot quicker and easier than
zh: 这可比从零开始

text: cooking from scratch, an idiom meaning to do something from the very beginning without using anything that's already been made.
zh: 做饭要快捷省事得多，"from scratch"是一个习语，意思是从最开始做起，不借助任何现成的东西。

text: Even though ultra processed foods are often unhealthy, they taste good.
zh: 尽管超加工食品通常不健康，但它们味道很好。

text: That's why Annabelle calls chocolate her guilty pleasure.
zh: 这就是为什么安娜贝尔把巧克力称为她的"罪恶快感"。

text: A guilty pleasure is something you enjoy but think you shouldn't and feel a little embarrassed
zh: "罪恶快感"指的是你很享受、但觉得不该做、因而略感尴尬的事情。

text: about. Ultra processed food is a tricky topic.
zh: 超加工食品是个棘手的话题。

text: We know these foods have been linked to poor health but at the same time they're cheap, convenient and taste good.
zh: 我们知道这些食品与健康不佳有关，但同时它们便宜、方便，味道也好。

text: So what should we do? Here's Hannah and Annabel discussing this for BBC programme What In The World.
zh: 那我们该怎么办呢？下面请听汉娜和安娜贝尔在BBC节目《What in the World》中的讨论。

text: Is it OK for me to have a packet of crisps once in a while?
zh: 我偶尔吃包薯片没关系吧？

text: Should we be cutting ultra processed foods out of our diets completely or is there a way for us to still enjoy them from time to time?
zh: 我们是应该把超加工食品完全排除在饮食之外，还是可以偶尔享用一下？

text: I think the best thing to do is just not to panic. Everything is fine in moderation.
zh: 我觉得最好的办法就是别恐慌。任何东西适量都没问题。

text: Hannah asks if it's OK to eat ultra -processed foods once in a while or from time to time.
zh: 汉娜问是否可以偶尔（once in a while）或时不时（from time to time）吃点超加工食品。

text: The phrases once in a while and from time to time mean occasionally. Sometimes, but not very often.
zh: "once in a while"和"from time to time"这两个短语的意思是偶尔，不常发生。

text: Annabel replies using the phrase everything in moderation, which advises us that it's best to avoid too much of anything.
zh: 安娜贝尔用"everything in moderation"这个短语回答，意思是任何东西都不宜过量。

text: That sounds sensible to me, and it also means I won't feel bad about eating chocolate
zh: 我觉得这话很有道理，这也意味着我以后

text: now and then. OK Pippa, it's time to reveal the answer to my question.
zh: 偶尔吃点巧克力也不用有负罪感了。好了Pippa，该公布我问题的答案了。

text: Now, I asked you when fizzy drinks were invented.
zh: 我之前问你碳酸饮料是什么时候发明的。

text: You said 1872. I'm afraid the correct answer was 1772.
zh: 你说是1872年。恐怕正确答案是1772年。

text: Apparently carbonated water was used to try to prevent scurvy on sea voyages.
zh: 据说当时人们用碳酸水来尝试预防海上航行中的坏血病。

text: Wow, that is amazing. I wouldn't have thought it was that long ago.
zh: 哇，太神奇了，我没想到会这么早。

text: Right, let's recap the vocabulary we've learnt, starting with ready meal –
zh: 好了，我们来回顾一下今天学到的词汇，首先是"ready meal"（即食餐）——

text: a meal from a supermarket that has already been prepared so you can heat it up quickly.
zh: 指超市里已经预先做好、可以快速加热食用的食品。

text: If you do something from scratch, you do it from the very beginning, without using anything that's already been made.
zh: 如果你"do something from scratch"，意思是你从最开始做起，不使用任何现成的东西。

text: A guilty pleasure is something you enjoy, but feel guilty or embarrassed about because you think you shouldn't do it.
zh: "guilty pleasure"（罪恶快感）指你很享受、但因为觉得不该做而感到内疚或尴尬的事情。

text: The phrases once in a while and from time to time mean occasionally, not very often.
zh: "once in a while"和"from time to time"这两个短语意思是偶尔，不常发生。

text: And finally, the phrase everything in moderation is used to advise someone that it's best to avoid too much of anything.
zh: 最后，"everything in moderation"这个短语用来建议人们，任何东西都不宜过量。

text: Once again, our six minutes are up, but if you're hungry for more, head over to our website bbclearningenglish .com
zh: 好了，我们的六分钟又到时间了，但如果你还意犹未尽，欢迎访问我们的网站bbclearningenglish.com，

text: for more tasty topics and useful vocabulary. See you again soon, but for now, it's goodbye.
zh: 获取更多有趣话题和实用词汇。我们下次再见，再见啦。

text: Bye! dot com
zh: 拜拜！dot com
